Event: Takes part in Season 2 paintball tournament while nursing newborn
Explanation: She risks some physical strain but gains competence and status while helping her team in the tournament.

Event: Stops negotiating and starts executing when stakes rise
Explanation: Her shift to decisive action tends to secure outcomes for herself and allies without clear harm.

Event: Alienates friends with Holier-than-Thou attitude and competitiveness
Explanation: Her moral superiority and competitive edge push friends away, hurting both her relationships and them socially.

Event: Starts drinking heavily after divorce
Explanation: She harms herself via alcoholism while creating concern and caretaking burdens for others.

Event: Almost backslides into drinking during weak moments
Explanation: Near-relapses threaten her recovery while prompting support/concern from friends who benefit from her staying sober.

Event: Alternate self falls fully off the wagon
Explanation: Her drinking devastates her own life while others only indirectly benefit by contrast/learning.

Event: Feeds “sexy dreadlocks guy” chicken fingers in exchange for company
Explanation: She gains brief romantic attention but is used for food, ultimately losing self-respect while he gets free meals.

Event: Sets group standards and declares what’s acceptable
Explanation: Her assumed authority gives her control and validation while constraining others’ autonomy and fun.

Event: Bullies and humiliates foosball competitors as a child
Explanation: She boosts her own status and satisfaction by cruelly humiliating others.

Event: Cooperates amicably with ex-husband post-divorce
Explanation: They maintain functional relations for their kids, benefiting both parents and children.

Event: Scolds group out of worry when they act recklessly
Explanation: Her angry concern protects others from harm and affirms her caring role with little downside.

Event: Strengthens religious faith to atone for past bullying and alcoholism
Explanation: Her guilt-driven faith stabilizes her and nudges her toward better behavior that modestly benefits others.

Event: Threatens Jeff with a pizza slicer
Explanation: Intimidation gives her minimal real gain while risking escalation and harming Jeff.

Event: Takes to video game cruelty and massacres NPC family
Explanation: She indulges in fun and catharsis by destroying innocent NPCs, providing almost no benefit to others.

Event: Starts drinking water instead of alcohol when out with group
Explanation: She preserves her sobriety and stability while easing group concern and avoiding drunk chaos.

Event: Passes shot of whiskey to Jeff instead of drinking it
Explanation: She protects herself from relapse while Jeff gets the drink; minimal harm to him in-story.

Event: Invites Annie to “pool party” that is an involuntary baptism
Explanation: She gains little real benefit while violating Annie’s autonomy and comfort.

Event: Attempts to force “What Would Baby Jesus Do?” bracelets on group
Explanation: She reinforces her religious norms while pressuring others in ways they don’t want.

Event: Trains Jeff brutally at foosball to win
Explanation: She satisfies her competitive drive and teaches Jeff, but the harshness and humiliation cost him.

Event: Keeps cooperating with Andre and eventually remarries him
Explanation: Their renewed partnership improves her family stability and emotional life as well as the kids’ wellbeing.

Event: Uses disappointed looks to control Jeff and the group
Explanation: She secures compliance and validation at the cost of others’ emotional comfort and autonomy.

Event: Maintains teetotal stance around drinking culture
Explanation: Abstaining protects her health and identity while modestly stabilizing group dynamics.

Event: Accidentally humiliates Jeff as a child until he wets his pants
Explanation: She gains power and amusement at the severe emotional expense of Jeff.

Event: Performs a Heel–Face Turn after childhood bullying incident
Explanation: Changing her behavior reduces her guilt and makes her kinder, benefitting her and others.

Event: Keeps functioning and studying while juggling divorce, parenting, and chaos
Explanation: Her persistence improves her prospects and maintains a baseline of care and stability for her family.

Event: Comes very close to storming off to guilt Jeff (pregnancy argument)
Explanation: She leverages her pregnancy to extract compliance, helping herself while pressuring Jeff emotionally.

Event: Forces beliefs on others early in the series
Explanation: She fortifies her moral authority but infringes on peers’ freedom and comfort.

Event: Invites group to “Comparative Religion” Christmas party under Christian defaults
Explanation: She gets to celebrate her traditions her way while making non-Christians uncomfortable.

Event: Gossips with Jeff in “Social Psychology”
Explanation: She and Jeff bond and gain entertainment at the reputational expense of others.

Event: Uses guilt-tripping to make group support her priorities
Explanation: Guilt yields cooperation and validation for her while burdening others emotionally.

Event: Uses religion as tool of superiority and manipulation
Explanation: She maintains a flattering self-image and control while alienating and pressuring others.

Event: Polices behavior based on selective moral myopia
Explanation: She preserves her ego and authority by double standards that constrain others more than herself.

Event: Rationalizes and excuses her own hypocrisy when called out
Explanation: She protects her self-image short-term but undermines growth and honesty, giving others only the benefit of clarity about her flaws.

Event: Actively works to reduce her bigotry and self-righteousness in Season 3
Explanation: She improves her own relationships and growth while making the group environment kinder.

Event: Remarries Andre partly for kids’ stability
Explanation: The remarriage strengthens her support system and creates a more stable home for the children.

Event: Uses passive-aggressive “sugary malice” to hurt while seeming nice
Explanation: She vents anger and exerts control while harming others emotionally.

Event: Threatens and attacks opponents in fights (e.g., Mike’s gang)
Explanation: She gains group safety and satisfaction at the physical and emotional expense of the bullies.

Event: Encourages Jeff to “kick Mike’s ass” after initially condemning violence
Explanation: She satisfies her vengeful impulses and supports Jeff, but escalates violence instead of reducing harm.

Event: Goes on video-game killing spree after initial moral objection
Explanation: She takes enjoyment and agency from extreme in-game violence that serves no one else.

Event: Uses food as bribe/leverage with the group
Explanation: Her cooking gains loyalty and influence while subtly pressuring others’ choices.

Event: Builds and runs “Shirley’s Sandwiches” business
Explanation: Entrepreneurship advances her goals and offers value to customers and community.

Event: Runs the sandwich shop at a loss until it fails
Explanation: She sacrifices time and money pursuing the business while providing some service but ultimately losing her marriage and kids.

Event: Agrees to remarry Andre the second time admitting fault
Explanation: Her accountability and renewed commitment benefit both her and Andre emotionally and practically.

Event: Leaves Greendale to care for her sick father
Explanation: She sacrifices campus life and perhaps career momentum to provide crucial family care.

Event: Represses rage under a smiling exterior
Explanation: Suppressing anger preserves her image but harms her mental health and indirectly strains relationships.

Event: Uses “mom voice” to shut down immoral or unsafe behavior
Explanation: She asserts control in ways that protect the group and reinforce her authority with low cost.

Event: Acts as Team Mom bringing food and caretaking “whether they want it or not”
Explanation: Her labor and support benefit her sense of purpose and the group’s wellbeing, even if sometimes overbearing.

Event: Uses “Playing the Family Card” to win arguments
Explanation: She secures help and deference through invoking motherhood, but it manipulates others’ feelings.

Event: Works excessively across school, parenting, and business responsibilities
Explanation: Hard work supports others and her reputation but significantly exhausts and strains her.
